facts.statement: Plate tectonics is the theory that Earth's outer shell is divided into large plates that move over the mantle.
questions: What is plate tectonics?
What does plate tectonics theory explain about Earth's structure?
How is Earth's outer shell organized according to plate tectonics?

facts.statement: The plate tectonics theory was developed in the 1960s.
questions: When was plate tectonics theory developed?
What decade did plate tectonics emerge as a theory?
In what time period was the theory of plate tectonics established?

facts.statement: Plate tectonics theory builds on Alfred Wegener's earlier idea of continental drift.
questions: What earlier theory did plate tectonics build upon?
Who proposed the idea of continental drift that influenced plate tectonics?
How does plate tectonics relate to continental drift?

facts.statement: Plates can move apart at divergent boundaries.
questions: What happens at divergent boundaries?
Where do plates move apart from each other?
What type of boundary features plates moving apart?

facts.statement: Plates can collide at convergent boundaries.
questions: What happens at convergent boundaries?
Where do tectonic plates collide?
What type of boundary involves plate collision?

facts.statement: Plates can slide past one another at transform boundaries.
questions: What happens at transform boundaries?
Where do plates slide past each other?
What type of boundary features plates sliding laterally?

facts.statement: The San Andreas Fault is a transform boundary.
questions: What type of plate boundary is the San Andreas Fault?
Is the San Andreas Fault a transform boundary?
What is an example of a transform boundary?

facts.statement: Most earthquakes occur along plate boundaries.
questions: Where do most earthquakes occur?
Why do earthquakes happen at plate boundaries?
What is the relationship between plate boundaries and earthquakes?

facts.statement: Most volcanoes occur along plate boundaries.
questions: Where do most volcanoes occur?
Why are volcanoes found at plate boundaries?
What is the relationship between plate boundaries and volcanoes?
